ActionScoreIn.description: One ranked action from a CDFL plan response — input to the emitter.
ActionScoreIn.title: ActionScoreIn

ActionScoreOut.description: One ranked action in the plan response.
ActionScoreOut.title: ActionScoreOut

dept_keys_to_skip.description: Skip these dept_keys; only honoured for is_required=FALSE.
dept_keys_to_skip.title: Dept Keys To Skip

dry_run.description: If TRUE, return preview WITHOUT writing departments/workflows.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

force.description: If TRUE and enterprise already bootstrapped, drop prior bootstrap row + redo. Destructive.
force.title: Force

BpmnDocOut.description: The BPMN 2.0 XML authored in the bpmn-js builder for a workflow.
BpmnDocOut.title: BpmnDocOut

BpmnSyncOut.description: Result of projecting the BPMN diagram onto workflow_nodes/edges.
BpmnSyncOut.title: BpmnSyncOut

ChatRequest.description:
A single user message. Stateless v0 — caller passes the visible
history each turn so the BE doesn't need a `chat_messages` table.

Conversation persistence is intentionally deferred to Phase 2
(F-NEW4). The trade-off is documented in the Sprint 8 plan: cost
of stateless = the FE has to keep a sessionStorage rolling buffer;
benefit = no migration, no RLS surface, no PII retention question.

history.description: Prior turns the FE wants the assistant to see. Capped at 20 turns to keep prompt size bounded.
history.title: History

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

billing_month.description: First day of the month to bill.
billing_month.title: Billing Month

CriticVerdict.description:
Output of the critic LLM after it reviews the executor transcript.

``action`` is the routing decision. The orchestrator dispatches:
    accept    → status=completed
    replan    → loop back to planner (until MAX_REPLAN)
    escalate  → status=escalated (surface to human)

``reason`` is the critic's narrative — what it judged and why. It
must be present (non-empty) regardless of the action so the
transcript explains the verdict.
CriticVerdict.title: CriticVerdict

CrossLinkEnrichedOut.description: Tree-viewer shape — adds display names + cross-dimension flags.
CrossLinkEnrichedOut.title: CrossLinkEnrichedOut

is_actioned.description: True flips the row to 'resolved' (counts toward North Star). False reverts (rare — usually only for typos).
is_actioned.title: Is Actioned

notes.description: Optional context — what action was taken or why the toggle was reverted.
notes.title: Notes

custom_message.description: Optional sender-supplied message rendered above the default copy in the email body.
custom_message.title: Custom Message

recipients.description: Email addresses to send this report to. De-duped + trimmed server-side; max 50 per call.
recipients.title: Recipients

EmitRequest.description:
Request body. Mirrors /cdfl/plan-next-action response so FE can
pipe one endpoint's output into the other.

intervention_channel.description: log_only = no external notification node; email/telegram/zalo emit external node with REL-012 compensation.
intervention_channel.title: Intervention Channel

workflow_name_suffix.description: Optional human-readable suffix for the workflow id. Allowed chars: [a-z0-9_-]. Auto-derived from current_state + top action when omitted.
workflow_name_suffix.title: Workflow Name Suffix

EmitRequest.title: EmitRequest

EmitResponse.description: Response shape. `yaml_parsed` lets the FE preview without YAML lib.
EmitResponse.title: EmitResponse

EnterpriseParentUpdate.description: Drag-drop re-parent payload — at most one of the three FKs at a time.
EnterpriseParentUpdate.title: EnterpriseParentUpdate

EventIn.description: One event for the inline mine request.
EventIn.title: EventIn

consent_external.description: K-4 — opt in to external LLM (Claude / GPT-4o) for this call. Default Qwen local.
consent_external.title: Consent External

period.description: Billing period this quota covers, e.g. '2026-05'.
period.title: Period

FrameworkRunListItem.description: List shape — content_json omitted to keep responses small.
FrameworkRunListItem.title: FrameworkRunListItem

next_cursor.description: Pass back as ``cursor`` query param to fetch the next page.
next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

InterventionPlanOut.description:
Resolved plan — channel + gate + rationale. FE/CSM tooling shows
rationale on the audit timeline.
InterventionPlanOut.title: InterventionPlanOut

InterventionTriggerRequest.description:
POST body. enterprise_id is NEVER trusted from here — read from
X-Enterprise-Id header (K-12).

intervention_type.description: Domain-specific type — 'csm_email', 'in_product_nudge', 'manager_callout', etc. Used for analytics + audit.
intervention_type.title: Intervention Type

pre_score.description: Composite adoption health score at trigger time.
pre_score.title: Pre Score

InterventionTriggerRequest.title: InterventionTriggerRequest

code.description: 6-digit TOTP code OR a backup code (10 chars).
code.title: Code

MineRequest.description:
Inline mine request — caller passes events directly.

For session-id-backed mode (load from Bronze) use `session_id` —
inline `events` MUST be empty in that case. Either mode required.
MineRequest.title: MineRequest

MinedWorkflowOut.description: Serialised MinedWorkflow. Tuple keys flattened to 'from|to' strings.
MinedWorkflowOut.title: MinedWorkflowOut

NOVCurrentResponse.description:
GET /economics/nov/current envelope.

Returns null `current` (HTTP 200, not 404) when no digest exists
yet — a brand-new tenant + workflow hasn't run; the dashboard
renders an empty state rather than an error tile.

classification.description: positive / negative / no_data — drives tile colour
classification.title: Classification

NOVCurrentResponse.title: NOVCurrentResponse

NOVMonthEntry.description: One month's NOV digest, formatted for the dashboard tile.
NOVMonthEntry.title: NOVMonthEntry

NOVTrendResponse.description: GET /economics/nov/trend envelope. Months are oldest → newest.
NOVTrendResponse.title: NOVTrendResponse

NodeTypeCatalogOut.description: One row of mig 068 node_type_catalog — drives builder palette + retry policy.
NodeTypeCatalogOut.title: NodeTypeCatalogOut

NorthStarTileResponse.description:
Dashboard tile payload — three numbers + a teaser of recently
resolved customers so the FE can render a sparkline / activity feed
without a second round-trip.

resolved_vnd.description: Canonical North Star number — sum where is_actioned=true. Renders as the headline.
resolved_vnd.title: Resolved Vnd

total_at_risk_vnd.description: Sum of revenue_at_risk across all flagged customers, actioned or not.
total_at_risk_vnd.title: Total At Risk Vnd

NorthStarTileResponse.title: NorthStarTileResponse

OnboardFromCsvRequest.description:
Bronze-tier file to read employees from. Column names default to
the canonical layout shipped in `data/sample/users-onboarding-approval.csv`
but can be overridden when an enterprise uses a different schema.

dry_run.description: When TRUE, no INSERT happens — the response describes what WOULD be created so the manager can preview before commit.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

OnboardFromCsvRequest.title: OnboardFromCsvRequest

override_value.description: Human-supplied corrected value the user thinks is right.
override_value.title: Override Value

reason.description: Why the AI's choice was wrong — feeds F-074 fine-tuning.
reason.title: Reason

reason.description: Optional — why the override is being revoked.
reason.title: Reason

PaletteNode.description: Single palette entry — catalog row + agent-specific bucket label.
PaletteNode.title: PaletteNode

PaletteResponse.description: Full palette grouped by bucket. FE renders bucket sections.
PaletteResponse.title: PaletteResponse

Plan.description:
Planner output. The orchestrator hands this to the executor
verbatim — no re-ordering, no per-step re-prompting.

``rationale`` is the planner's overall narrative ("I chose to fetch
customer details first so I can personalise the draft email"). It
does NOT influence execution but reads well in the transcript.
Plan.title: Plan

PlanRequest.description:
Plan request. Format mirrors /process-mining/mine output exactly so
FE can pipe one endpoint's response into the other.

`direct_follows` keys are "from|to" delimited strings (same convention
as MinedWorkflowOut). Counts must be positive ints.

`current_state` is the event_type the agent is currently "standing at" —
typically the most-recent event in a customer journey or the latest
process step that completed.
PlanRequest.title: PlanRequest

PlanResponse.description:
Top-K actions ranked by CDFL information gain (descending).

`current_state` echoed for FE convenience.
`theory_position` line is the honest niche statement from luận văn —
surfaced so the UI tooltip can show "this is CDFL's research position"
without claiming SOTA.

theory_position.default: CDFL (Convergent Dual-Field Learning) ranking — bounded compute exploration with scaling advantage in tabular environments. Niche per NNL-NTHT 8-phase benchmark; not SOTA RL replacement.
theory_position.title: Theory Position

PlanResponse.title: PlanResponse

PlanStep.description:
One executable step in the plan.

``tool_name`` must match a tool in the chat registry (the same
registry the chat agent uses). ``args`` is the JSON arg payload
the LLM produced; the executor still strips forbidden keys (K-12 /
K-16) before dispatch.

``rationale`` is the planner's per-step justification. Surfaced in
the transcript so a human auditor can see WHY the planner picked
each step, not just what it did.
PlanStep.title: PlanStep

RAGAnswerRequest.description:
POST /rag/answer body. tenant_id is NEVER read from here — K-12.
The router extracts it from the X-Enterprise-Id header.

engines_whitelist.description: Optional per-call whitelist. Tenant-level whitelist in tenant_settings.rag_engines takes precedence; this is a narrowing override (intersection).
engines_whitelist.title: Engines Whitelist

RAGAnswerRequest.title: RAGAnswerRequest

RAGAnswerResponse.description:
Engine response envelope. FE renders `answer` as the chat reply
and `citations` as the source trail.
RAGAnswerResponse.title: RAGAnswerResponse

RAGCitationOut.description:
One citation. Per-engine fields are optional so FE renders only
the populated ones.
RAGCitationOut.title: RAGCitationOut

ReportDetail.description:
Full shape including the validated content_json. Returned by the
detail endpoint.
ReportDetail.title: ReportDetail

ReportListItem.description:
Trimmed shape for the list view — content_json is omitted to
keep the response under a few KB regardless of report size.
ReportListItem.title: ReportListItem

next_cursor.description: Pass back as ``cursor`` query param to fetch the next page. Absent when no more rows.
next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

RevenueEstimateRequest.description:
POST /economics/revenue/estimate body. Method discriminates which
inputs are read; the others are ignored. Decimal-as-str on the way
in (precision-safe — see _to_month_entry rationale).
RevenueEstimateRequest.title: RevenueEstimateRequest

RoleChangeRequest.description:
Either template-resolved OR explicit-override. Exactly one path
must be present:

* template path  — provide `department_id + seniority_level`.
* override path  — provide `role`.

Sending both = 422 (the FE is meant to pick one). Sending neither =
422 (nothing to do).
RoleChangeRequest.title: RoleChangeRequest

is_override.description: TRUE when this row was an enterprise-specific override (enterprise_id matched). FALSE when the resolver fell back to a global default row.
is_override.title: Is Override

RoleTemplateResolveOut.description:
Response shape for the dept-scoped resolve endpoint. Bundles the
chosen template plus the dept info the FE needs to render the
"đề xuất quyền: MANAGER" hint next to the approve button.
RoleTemplateResolveOut.title: RoleTemplateResolveOut

RunCreateRequest.description: Tier-aware union shape — service layer validates per tier.
RunCreateRequest.title: RunCreateRequest

SessionRequest.description:
POST /api/v1/shared/agents/sessions body.

``input`` shape varies per workflow — validated against the
workflow's input_schema (workflows.py) at the orchestrator layer
rather than at the FastAPI surface, because the validator needs
access to the registry. Letting the wrong shape through here would
just bounce off the orchestrator with a 400.

dry_run.description: When TRUE (default), action tools record what they WOULD have done but skip side-effects. Set FALSE to actually fire emails, mark customers, etc. Requires Idempotency-Key header at the router layer when FALSE.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

workflow_id.description: ID of a built-in workflow. v0: 'insight-to-action'. Tenant-defined workflows are not supported.
workflow_id.title: Workflow Id

SessionRequest.title: SessionRequest

SessionResponse.description:
POST /api/v1/shared/agents/sessions response body.

A snapshot of the session at the moment the orchestrator finished.
Status is one of the terminal values (completed | failed | escalated)
OR a transient one if the caller polled mid-run (planning |
executing | critiquing — only relevant for follow-up GET endpoint
that we don't ship in PR1).
SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse

SimulateRequest.description:
What-if scenario request. period_label identifies the baseline
digest row to load; all change fields default to 0 so the request
is partial-friendly.
SimulateRequest.title: SimulateRequest

SourceRef.description: One entry in `source_ids` for intermediate tier.
SourceRef.title: SourceRef

api_key.description: Raw API key — server encrypts before persist.
api_key.title: Api Key

TenantSettingsIn.description:
Subset of tenant settings the resolver needs. Caller (CSM tooling)
loads from tenant config + posts; we don't read DB here so the
endpoint stays pure-function testable.
TenantSettingsIn.title: TenantSettingsIn

TranscriptEntry.description:
Snapshot of one ``agent_transcripts`` row, as returned by the API.

Persistence row has more fields (latency_ms, llm_tokens, ...);
those are shipped over the wire only when the caller asks for
them via a follow-up detail endpoint. Keeping the response slim
by default.
TranscriptEntry.title: TranscriptEntry

WorkflowRunCreate.description: Body for POST /workflows/{id}/run. enterprise_id from JWT header only.
WorkflowRunCreate.title: WorkflowRunCreate

WorkflowStopAction.description: Body for POST /workflow-runs/{run_id}/stop (K-23 — human can stop a run).
WorkflowStopAction.title: WorkflowStopAction

WorkflowTreeOut.description: Hierarchical shape for FE tree viewer.
WorkflowTreeOut.title: WorkflowTreeOut

_VarianceInputs.description: NOV-REV-006 — predicted vs actual variance check.
_VarianceInputs.title: _VarianceInputs

consent_external.description: K-4 — must be explicit per call. Default OFF (Qwen local).
consent_external.title: Consent External

question.description: The question / hypothesis the framework should structure.
question.title: Question

source_ref.description: Optional pointer into the data layer (gold feature id, analysis_run id, dataset name) for the prompt to ground on.
source_ref.title: Source Ref

owner_email.description: Where to send the report-ready notification.
owner_email.title: Owner Email

params.description: Opaque caller params forwarded to the template's system prompt (period, dataset filter, etc.).
params.title: Params

title.description: User-facing title for the FE list view + email subject.
title.title: Title

info.description: Analysis execution, strategy frameworks, dashboard, insights
info.title: Kaori AI Orchestrator

get.description: Per-source dead/failed counts. Op uses this as a dashboard.

post.description:
Flip a rejected intake row back to pending for re-claim.

K-13: Idempotency-Key dedupes double-click. Without it a second
requeue could land while the row is mid-claim, racing the claimant.

Permanently drop a dead notification — no retry. Caller's explicit
decision (record in audit log).

K-13: discard is destructive; Idempotency-Key prevents a double-click
from deleting one row then 404-ing on the second call.

Flip dead → pending so the poller picks it back up.

K-13: pass Idempotency-Key header; double-click from ops dashboard
returns the original response instead of re-flipping a row that
has since changed status.

Resume a failed run from where it stopped. The runner's resume-aware
loop preloads completed node outputs + skips them; only failed/pending
nodes re-execute. Idempotent — repeated calls are no-ops if run is
already running or completed.

K-13: Idempotency-Key header dedupes double-click; first call records
409 outcome in ledger; subsequent calls within 24h return cached 409
so background replay only schedules once per click.

schema.title: Response List Dlq Items Admin Dlq  Source  Get
Response List Incidents Admin Incidents Get

get.description:
Art 72 monitoring single-pane: open incidents by severity + recent
failed runs + recent low-confidence decisions.

Note: workflow_runs has no `created_at` — its creation timestamp column is
`started_at` (mig 088). The interval cast follows the repo idiom
(chat/tools/platform.py): ``($N::text || ' days')::interval``.

post.description:
Find all terminal runs in the last `hours` + reconcile each.
Returns aggregate counts + per-run detail.

Re-INSERT any missing workflow_run_nodes rows for this run from
the workflow_events log. Idempotent: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

AI-HSC-016 — rank a tenant's health metric against an anonymised
peer cohort.

Resolve the per-tenant plan + capture the baseline.

Fails-CLOSED on misconfig (I1 self-review fix): if the tenant set
`requires_manager_approval=true` without binding Telegram, the
resolver raises and we return 422 — CSM tooling must reconcile
before retrying. Auto-firing the intervention would silently bypass
the audit gate the tenant explicitly opted into (K-6 spirit).
Free-form AI question — does NOT route to a named framework.

get.description: Return top 3 data-driven recommendations based on latest analysis results.

Workspaces the calling user can include in a cross-cohort.

PR B "lite" honours the Phase 1 model — one user belongs to exactly
one enterprise — so this returns a single-item list with the user's
real role from the JWT claims. When `user_workspace_memberships`
lands (PR D), this query expands to walk every membership the user
has ANALYST+ on.

Real external-AI call quota for the current month — counts
every ``decision_audit_log`` row tagged ``llm_provider != qwen-internal``
since the first of the month. Limit is hardcoded to 100 in PR B
(Phase 1 plans table doesn't carry the field yet — F-067 wires it).
Cursor-paginated list. Cursor format: ``<created_at_iso>|<run_id>``.

post.description:
Queue a tier run + spawn the background dispatcher. Returns 202
+ run_id; the FE polls the GET endpoint until status='done' or
'error'.

get.description:
Single tier run including overview / templates / config. RLS
ensures cross-tenant requests get 404.

post.description:
Approve a pending advanced-tier run. MANAGER role required —
the JWT enforcement at the gateway already restricts the path,
but we double-check here so a misconfigured filter can't slip
a non-MANAGER through to a privacy-sensitive dispatch.

get.description:
Picker catalogue for the intermediate tier left pane. Layer
filter is FE-driven so users can scope to silver or gold only.

parameters.description: Comma-separated layers — silver, gold, or both. Default: silver,gold.
parameters.name: layer

schema.description: Comma-separated layers — silver, gold, or both. Default: silver,gold.
schema.title: Layer

get.description: List recent analysis runs for this enterprise (K-1: enterprise filter).

post.description:
Create an analysis_run record and queue execution.
Returns immediately (202 Accepted); poll /runs/{id} for status.

get.description: Get analysis run status + per-template results.
Return all templates with eligibility flag for the given data profile.

Pending approvals awaiting THIS caller across every run — the approver
inbox. Filters to rows where the caller's role is in approver_roles (or they
are the pinned approver). No role header → all pending (manager/admin view).
Carries run/workflow context + SLA remaining + chain level.
Return quota usage for current billing period.

post.description: Rank candidate next actions using CDFL information gain.

Enterprise-scoped chat. Tools see ``ctx.enterprise_id`` from the
gateway-trusted header and run RLS-scoped queries against the
caller's tenant only.

Platform-scoped chat. Cross-tenant queries — gated to platform
admin roles. The role check happens here AND in the registry, so
a future endpoint refactor can't accidentally bypass it.

get.description: Latest classification for a workflow, or null.

schema.title: Response Get Latest For Workflow Compliance Ai Uses Get

post.description: Classify an AI-use into a risk_tier; auto-derive controls; audit (K-6).

Internal click-to-sign. Appends an immutable signature; when the required
signatures land, the contract becomes 'hiệu lực' and (if it was produced by a
contract node) resumes the paused workflow run.

delete.description:
Soft delete — sets status='archived'. Hard delete is intentionally
not supported (would cascade-orphan subsidiaries; do that via the
enterprise re-parent endpoint first).

get.description:
Flat list of all org nodes (group → division → enterprise) for
the caller's workspace. FE renders the tree by parent_id linking.

schema.title: Response List Tree Corporate Tree Get
Response List Customer Contracts Customer Contracts Get

get.description:
Cursor-paginated list of at-risk customers (revenue_at_risk > 0)
for the FE to render the action UI. Sorted by revenue_at_risk DESC
so the highest-impact customers surface first.

Cursor format: base64url(``<revenue_at_risk>|<customer_external_id>``).

post.description:
Mark / unmark a customer's ``is_actioned`` flag on gold_features.

Idempotent: same (customer, is_actioned) hit twice writes the same
row state but bumps actioned_at — the FE shows the latest toggle
time even if the value didn't change. Kafka emit fires every time
(including no-op flips) so consumers see the user touched the row.
Best-effort emit; a Kafka outage doesn't roll back the DB write.

get.description:
Three-number tile for the home dashboard.

Formula matches CLAUDE.md §14:
    SUM(revenue_at_risk WHERE churn_risk_label='HIGH' AND is_actioned=true)

v0 uses ``revenue_at_risk > 0`` as the HIGH proxy (the aggregator
only writes non-zero for model-flagged customers; idle customers
stay at 0). F-051 explicit classifier later narrows to a label
column.

Returns one of 5 states so the frontend can render the correct view:
  no_data → first_upload → pending_review → analysis_ready → results_ready
List decision audit rows for the calling tenant, newest first.

Stream a UTF-8-BOM CSV of decision rows. Capped at 10 000 rows; when
the cap fires we set ``X-Export-Truncated: true`` so the FE can warn.

BOM (``\xef\xbb\xbf``) is required for Vietnamese Excel — without it
Excel guesses Windows-1252 and renders mojibake on diacritics.

Single decision row + action toggle + override history.

RLS scopes the SELECT — cross-tenant requests get the same 404 as
a missing decision_id. The override list is ordered newest-first so
the FE can pick ``[0]`` for the currently effective override (with
``revoked_at IS NULL``).

post.description: Mark / unmark a decision as actioned. Idempotent UPSERT.

Append a new override row + emit kaori.feedback.actions.

Append-only: an override on a decision that already has overrides
is fine — the latest non-revoked one is the "current" effective
override. The audit trail keeps the full history for forensics +
fine-tuning data exports.

Soft-revoke a prior override. The row stays for forensics; the
FE filters by ``revoked_at IS NULL`` to find the currently
effective override.

Re-revoking an already-revoked override is a no-op (returns 409)
rather than overwriting the original revoke metadata — preserves
"first revoke wins" semantics for audit clarity.

get.description:
List active departments for the current enterprise. RLS scopes
via acquire_for_tenant — MANAGER sees own enterprise only.

post.description:
Create a department under the caller's enterprise. enterprise_id
comes from JWT-forwarded X-Enterprise-ID (K-12), never from body.

get.description:
Department lookup. Workspace-scoped — Vingroup HQ user can read any
department in the same workspace (VinMart, VinFast, …). Used by the FE
department-workflows page to show dept name + parent enterprise.

Resolve `(dept_type, seniority_level) → default_role` for an
onboarding approval flow. FE calls this when the approver opens the
"duyệt nhân viên" drawer; the response drives the "Đề xuất quyền"
hint + the writer payload when approve is clicked.

Resolution order:
  1. Enterprise-specific override for (enterprise_id, dept_type,
     seniority).
  2. Global default for (dept_type, seniority).
  3. 404 — caller must pick a role manually.
Direct child folders of `parent_id` (or root when omitted). Keyset-paginated.

delete.description: Soft-delete a folder + its subtree (legal/audit — never hard-delete).

get.description: Ancestor chain root→folder (for the breadcrumb), derived from the slug path.
Indexed search across the repository (name + type + status), not tree-walk.

post.description:
NOV-CST-012 — straight-line amortization of one-time setup cost.

Body returns the full schedule (monthly allocation + cumulative +
remaining) so the CFO digest can render the table without a second
call. Pure function — no DB access; tenant header parsed for audit.

get.description:
Latest digest for the tenant + classification hint.

Tenant scoping: the gateway-supplied ``X-Enterprise-Id`` header
populates the RLS GUC via ``acquire_for_tenant``. K-12 — header
extracted from JWT claims at the gateway, never trusted from the
body / query string.

Last N months of digests, oldest → newest. Front-end plots
left-to-right; the persistence layer reverses order before return.

parameters.description: How many months of history to return.
parameters.name: months

schema.description: How many months of history to return.
schema.title: Months

get.description:
NOV-RPT-020 — CFO-style quarterly digest.

Aggregates 3 monthly digests + QoQ + YoY comparisons. Phase 1.5 only
supports period='quarterly'; monthly/annual variants land Phase 2.

parameters.description: ISO-ish 'YYYY-Qn' label. Defaults to the quarter containing today.
parameters.name: quarter

schema.description: ISO-ish 'YYYY-Qn' label. Defaults to the quarter containing today.
schema.title: Quarter

parameters.description: Monthly-amortized setup cost to fold into the digest (decimal-as-str). Caller computes via /economics/cost/compute then passes the result here.
parameters.name: setup_monthly_vnd

schema.description: Monthly-amortized setup cost to fold into the digest (decimal-as-str). Caller computes via /economics/cost/compute then passes the result here.
schema.title: Setup Monthly Vnd

get.description:
NOV-RPT-023 — top-K underperforming workflows + suggested
replacement templates + blocked OKRs.

Triggered when CFO digest shows negative NOV. Pure read endpoint —
suggestions are advisory, not auto-applied.

schema.title: Response Manager Digest Recommendations Economics Reports Manager Digest Recommendations Get

post.description:
NOV-RPT-024 — what-if simulation of NOV under a scenario change.

Loads the baseline monthly digest by period_label, applies the
scenario, returns projected NOV + 95% CI + assumptions.

Dispatch to one of 3 revenue estimators (NOV-REV-001/002/003).

Pure function — no DB access. Tenant header is still parsed for
audit logging + future per-tenant override hooks (vendor cap, etc.).

schema.title: Response List Billing Lines Economics Roi Billing Lines Get

post.description:
SH-M59-001 — synchronously compute + persist the billing line
for the calling tenant for the given month. Idempotent: re-running
skips an existing line for the same (enterprise_id, billing_month).

Phase 2.5+ moves this onto a platform-admin endpoint that iterates
all opted-in tenants in one run.

SH-M59-004 — opt the calling tenant into ENT ROI tier. Eligibility
(≥3 months of data, SH-M59-005) is reported but not blocking — the
monthly cron will skip charging until it confirms.
Opt back out. Existing billing lines stay untouched (K-2).

Compute without persisting. Useful for FE "how much would I
have been charged?" widgets.

Bulk-onboard employees from a previously uploaded CSV.

Per row:
  1. Extract email + full_name + department + seniority_level.
  2. Classify the dept name (Vietnamese-aware) → dept_type enum.
  3. Resolve department_id by (enterprise_id, dept_type, status='active').
  4. Look up department_role_templates → default_role.
  5. INSERT enterprise_users with status='pending' + random unusable
     BCrypt hash. ON CONFLICT (enterprise_id, email) DO NOTHING.
  6. Audit row: enterprise_user.onboarded.

Errors per row do NOT abort the batch — outcomes[] reports each one
so the manager can fix and re-upload. On `dry_run=true` no INSERT
happens; outcome='created' on dry_run means "would create".

patch.description:
Apply a role change to a target user. Either template-derived or
explicit override; either way the audit row says which.

The caller's X-User-Role header is what the gateway forwards from
the JWT — auth-service signs JWTs whose claims include `role`. In
local-dev runs where the gateway is bypassed, MANAGER is hard-required.

post.description:
Clone industry template → enterprise's departments + workflows + KPI cache.

K-12 anti-IDOR: enterprise_id in path MUST match X-Enterprise-ID header.
Idempotent via enterprise_industry_bootstrap UNIQUE(enterprise_id); pass
force=true to drop+re-bootstrap (destructive).

get.description:
Return the branches + departments under an enterprise.

The FE org tree page calls this when the user expands an enterprise
leaf to show its internal structure (chi nhánh + phòng ban). Sits at
the bottom of v_corporate_tree (group → division → enterprise) — we
drop down two more levels here.

Authz: caller's enterprise must share workspace with the target
enterprise. Otherwise 403.

put.description:
Re-parent an enterprise within the org tree.

Exactly one of the three FK fields determines the new parent:
  - corporate_group_id   → enterprise sits directly under the group
                            (no division — for ungrouped subsidiaries)
  - business_division_id → enterprise sits under a division
  - parent_enterprise_id → enterprise is a sub-subsidiary of another
                            enterprise (e.g. VinFast Auto under VinFast)

Validation:
  - target FK must point at a node in the same workspace
  - parent_enterprise_id must not create a cycle through this enterprise

get.description:
Cursor-paginated list of framework runs for the calling tenant.
Cursor format: ``<created_at_iso>|<run_id>``.

post.description:
Queue a framework run. Returns 202 + ``run_id``; the actual
LLM call runs in a background asyncio task. Poll the GET endpoint
until status='ready' or 'failed'.

get.description:
Static catalogue of built-in frameworks. Used by the FE hub
page (file 40-frameworks.tsx) to render the gallery without
duplicating the registry on the client side.

Single framework run including content_json. RLS ensures
cross-tenant requests get 404.

K8s readiness probe target. Returns 200 with detail when
healthy or deliberately disabled; 503 when the worker should be
running but the cluster reports no pollers.

schema.title: Response Temporal Health Health Temporal Get

get.description: List all active industries from `v_industry_overview` (Phase 2.8 — currently 3 of 8 seeded).
Full detail: industry + depts + workflows + KPIs + schemas + roles.

schema.title: Response Get Industry Detail Industries  Industry Id  Get
Response List Industry Departments Industries  Industry Id  Departments Get
Response List Industry Workflows Industries  Industry Id  Workflows Get

get.description:
Return AI-generated insight cards from latest analysis results.
Each card: {id, title, body, category, run_id}
List knowledge visible to the tenant: global (tier 1-3) + own (tier 4).

post.description:
Ingest one tenant-specific (tier 4) knowledge doc. Embedded synchronously
so it is searchable immediately.
Semantic search over global + own knowledge (cosine over BGE-M3).

get.description:
Walk the lineage graph forward from (kind, object_id). Answers
'what did this object produce?'

Walk the lineage graph backward from (kind, object_id). Answers
'where did this object come from?'
Endpoint that serves Prometheus metrics.

post.description:
Run the field-key re-encrypt worker for the calling tenant.
Walks every registered encrypted column, decrypts via key history,
rewrites under the current key version. Idempotent — safe to call
repeatedly; rows already under current key are skipped.

Synchronous in this Phase 2 implementation. Phase 2.5+ moves the
work onto a Temporal worker (TEMPORAL_ENABLE_WORKER=true) for
tenants with high row counts.

K-13: re-encrypt is natural-idempotent (rows already current skip)
but worker invocation is heavy (scans all encrypted columns).
Idempotency-Key dedupes worker runs from double-click on admin panel
so we don't pay the scan cost twice.

get.description:
Report the tenant's current re-encrypt worker state. Returns
404 if the tenant has no field-encryption key provisioned yet.

post.description:
Rotate the tenant's field-encryption key. Increments version
and archives the prior key into tenant_field_key_versions so
existing ciphertext stays decryptable until the re-encrypt worker
(POST /field-key/reencrypt) rewrites every row under the new key.

K-13: rotation writes to Vault under prod profile — wasteful double
Vault writes from double-click would litter the secret store with
abandoned keys. Idempotency-Key collapses to 1 rotation.

Lifecycle:
  1. Capture current (version, key_ref) — both old + new go into
     tenant_field_key_versions so history fallback can decrypt
     pre-rotation ciphertext.
  2. Bump version on tenant_field_keys; install new key_ref.
  3. Mark reencrypt_status='pending' so the worker (manual trigger
     or scheduled) picks the tenant up.

First call for a new tenant inserts version=1 (no rotation; just
provisions the initial key) and skips the pending flag.

In dev profile, generates a fresh inline:base64 key. Production
swaps to writing to Vault path.

delete.description:
Disable MFA — wipes secret + all backup codes. Caller is
expected to require a recent re-verification before invoking this
(frontend gate); router does NOT re-check here for simplicity.

post.description:
Generate a fresh TOTP secret + 10 backup codes. Stores
encrypted secret + hashed codes; returns the secret (base32 +
otpauth URL) and PLAINTEXT codes so the user can save them.

Re-enroll overwrites existing secret. enabled flips back to FALSE
until /verify succeeds.

K-13: HIGH severity — double-enroll overwrites secret + drops
backup codes. Idempotency-Key header returns the original
enrollment payload (with the same secret + codes the caller
already saved) instead of generating a fresh one. Cached 24h.

Verify a TOTP code (6 digits) OR a backup code (10 alphanumeric).

On first successful TOTP verify after /enroll, flips enabled=TRUE.
Backup-code verify marks the code as used (single-use).

K-13: backup-code verify burns the code (single-use); double-click
on slow network could fire 2 calls. DB-level natural dedup wins the
race (`WHERE used_at IS NULL` returning rows), but client sees only
one success. Idempotency-Key caches the response so both replies
are identical — UX clean.

INTERNAL — auth-service Java side calls this to translate an
sso_code into the matched user, then mints a real JWT and returns
it to the FE.

Requires X-Internal-Service-Token == KAORI_INTERNAL_SVC_TOKEN env
var. The token is a shared secret between the two services; rotate
via Vault path `platform/auth/internal_service_token` Phase 2.5+.

get.description:
Handle the provider's redirect after user authorization.

Validates state (CSRF), exchanges code for userinfo, matches by
email to an enterprise_users row, persists/refreshes the sso_identity,
issues a one-shot exchange code, redirects the browser to
return_url with ?sso_code=<code>.

Browser flow → FE picks up sso_code → POSTs to auth-service
/auth/sso/exchange (Java side TO BUILD) → gets JWT.

Issue a state token and return the provider's authorization URL.
FE redirects the browser there next.

Pre-auth endpoint — no tenant header required (we don't know which
enterprise the user belongs to until callback resolves the email).
List OKRs with optional period / status / department filter.

schema.title: Response List Okrs P2 Strategy Okr Get

post.description:
Create OKR + optional inline KRs. Progress recomputed on commit.

K-13: pass Idempotency-Key header; double-click on save button returns
the original OKR (with the same okr_id) instead of creating a 2nd row.

get.description: Detail with KRs + linked workflows.

post.description: K-13: Idempotency-Key dedupes double-click add-KR.

Link a workflow to this OKR with a contribution weight.

K-13: DB-level UNIQUE(workflow_id, okr_id) already prevents duplicate;
Idempotency-Key returns the original link payload so client gets the
same 201 on retry (instead of 409 from UNIQUE conflict).

schema.title: Response List Tenant Keys Platform Llm Api Keys Get

post.description:
Encrypt the raw API key with the tenant's field-encryption key
(mig 074 dogfood) + upsert one row per (tenant, provider).

K-13: financial impact — wrong-clicking "rotate" twice would not
create 2 vendor keys (UPSERT clamps to 1 row), but billing audit log
would show 2 rotations. Idempotency-Key collapses to 1 audit entry.

get.description:
Global provider catalog. Tenant header still required (K-12)
for audit trail.

schema.title: Response List Providers Platform Llm Catalog Providers Get

get.description:
List platform AI tuning knobs (RAG / memory / grounding / embedding).
SUPER_ADMIN only. `applied=false` knobs are surfaced but not yet wired.

schema.title: Response List Ai Config Platform Llm Config Get

patch.description:
Update one knob. SUPER_ADMIN only. Validates against the row's declared
type + min/max. Invalidates the ai_config cache so the change takes effect
within seconds (TTL).

schema.title: Response Token Breakdown Platform Llm Tokens Breakdown Get
Response List Upgrade Tests Platform Llm Versions Upgrade Test Get

post.description:
Start a 90-day shadow A/B test (P1-LLM-006). The candidate
runs alongside `current_model` — the orchestrator decides whether
to mirror calls to it based on tenant's `shadow_sample_rate` setting
(default 5% — implemented in llm-gateway adapter, not here).

K-13: Idempotency-Key dedupes double-click; without it, 2 RUNNING
test rows for same (tenant, candidate) would compete for shadow traffic.

K-13: promote is IRREVERSIBLE in business terms (new baseline starts
billing). Idempotency-Key prevents double-fire 404 (2nd call would see
status=PROMOTED + return 404 with WHERE status='RUNNING').

K-13: same dedupe pattern as promote — 2nd reject click after status
flipped REJECTED would see 404.

get.description: Project resource usage `horizon_days` ahead via linear regression.

Detect anomalies in a recent window of a metric's time series.

Phase 1.5 reads from api_request_log + etl_run_log aggregations.
Phase 2 will plumb through OTel/Prometheus directly.

schema.title: Response Detect Metric Anomalies Platform Observability Metric Anomalies Get

get.description:
Return current consent state for the calling user, or null if
no consent record exists yet (implies not-opted-in).

schema.title: Response Get Consent Platform Observability Sessions Consent Get

post.description: Grant or revoke session-replay consent for the calling user.

Submit a redacted session recording. Server re-applies redaction
(defense-in-depth) before persisting.

get.description:
Fetch the recorded event stream. Returns redacted events
(K-5) — the raw was never stored.

post.description:
Run Heuristic Miner over inline events; return discovered workflow.

Build Week demo flow:
    FE uploads CSV → BE normalises to events → POST here →
    rendered as graph on Process Mining canvas screen.

Session-id mode is reserved for post-Build-Week. Returns 501 if used.
PM-ANM-023..027 — run 5 anomaly detectors over inline events.
PM-ALG-016/017 — dispatch one of 3 algorithms over inline events.

Route + dispatch a RAG query.

Default heuristic dispatch per RAG_ADDENDUM_2026_05.md:
  - doc-citation pattern → pageindex
  - short insight query → pgvector
  - long multi-entity question → docsage
  - default → pgvector

Tenant whitelist (tenant_settings.rag_engines) restricts which engines
can answer. If every whitelisted engine raises NotImplementedError
(DocSage stub today), responds 503 rather than silently bypassing
the policy (R1 self-review fix).

ranking=cdfl_ig: bypass heuristic dispatch + force PageIndex path,
then re-rank leaves via CDFLRagReranker. Subsequent calls for the
same tenant accumulate session state — leaves visited many times
lose IG; unexplored leaves get an uncertainty bonus. Phase 1.5
in-memory only.

parameters.description: default = heuristic engine dispatch; cdfl_ig = PageIndex tree leaves re-ranked by CDFL information gain (novelty + uncertainty per NNL-NTHT, in-memory per-tenant session model).
parameters.name: ranking

schema.description: default = heuristic engine dispatch; cdfl_ig = PageIndex tree leaves re-ranked by CDFL information gain (novelty + uncertainty per NNL-NTHT, in-memory per-tenant session model).
schema.title: Ranking

get.description:
Cursor-paginated list of reports for the calling tenant.
Cursor format: ``<created_at_iso>|<report_id>`` so the next page
can resume after the last row deterministically.

Single report including content_json. RLS ensures cross-tenant
requests return 404 (the row simply isn't visible).

post.description:
Manually distribute a ready report to additional recipients.
Returns 202 + a per-recipient summary. Each recipient gets one
notification_outbox row (poller handles SMTP retries) and one
report_distributions audit row.

Status codes:
  * 404 — report not found / not visible to tenant
  * 409 — report exists but status != 'ready'
  * 400 — empty / over-cap recipients

get.description:
List manual distributions for a report, joined with the live
notification_outbox state (status / attempts / sent_at). Used by
the FE distribution audit drawer.

List role templates visible to the caller.

Default returns 35 global rows. When `include_overrides=true` and the
caller's enterprise has overrides, those shadow the globals in the
response (only the override row is returned per `(dept_type,
seniority)` pair). Set `include_overrides=false` to always read the
raw global defaults (admin use case).

schema.title: Response List Role Templates Role Templates Get

post.description:
Start a new agent session for ``body.workflow_id``.

Validates the workflow + input shape, then drives the
planner/executor/critic loop synchronously. Returns
SessionResponse with the full transcript when the loop terminates
(completed | failed | escalated).

get.description:
Return curated palette for the agent workflow builder.

Reads mig 068 node_type_catalog and joins with the AGENT_PALETTE_BUCKETS
curation. Tenant-isolated via JWT header (K-12) though the catalog
itself is global (no tenant_id column) — `acquire_for_tenant` still
enforces the GUC for downstream RLS calls in same session.

parameters.description: Filter to one bucket. Omit to get full palette.
parameters.name: bucket

schema.description: Filter to one bucket. Omit to get full palette.
schema.title: Bucket

post.description:
Same orchestrator, workflow_id pinned by the URL.

Body's ``workflow_id`` field is ignored — the path always wins.
This makes the URL the source of truth for which workflow ran,
which is what audit / dashboards expect.
Route question to exactly 1 framework (K-10), run it, return structured result.

schema.title: Response List Vendor Contracts Vendor Contracts Get

get.description:
List cross-workflow links visible to the caller. When workflow_id
is supplied, returns only links touching that workflow (in or out).

schema.title: Response List Cross Links Workflow Cross Links Get

post.description:
Declare a cross-workflow dependency.

Per anh 2026-05-15: 1 phòng ban có nhiều workflow; workflow phòng A
có thể liên quan workflow phòng B của công ty khác / chi nhánh khác /
mảng khác. This endpoint records the link statically; Phase 2 Temporal
runtime will fire on link_type='triggers'.

get.description: Latest analysis for a document, or {status:'never_run'}.

post.description:
Analyze an uploaded document (summary + key fields + risks). Async — the
LLM runs off the request path; poll GET …/analysis for the result.

patch.description:
Set the class + requirement on a freshly-uploaded attachment (the upload
path created the row; this gives it meaning in the tree).

get.description:
Serve the raw file bytes for a workflow document (ADR-0037 Phase 0).
Resolves attachment → bronze file → pipeline_runs (sha256 + mime), reads the
content-addressed blob. RLS-scoped; the key uses the caller's enterprise.

post.description:
Register a new version (already-uploaded bronze file_id) that supersedes
this document: the old row goes is_current=FALSE + superseded_by=new; the new
row inherits class/requirement, version+1, status='da_nop' (re-review).

Advance a document through the 7-state machine. Reject / request-more
require a note; approve/reject/request-more stamp the reviewer.

Ingest a form submission. Workflows with read_form_submission node
pick this up on next run.

get.description:
List the 45-row node_type_catalog (mig 068). FE builder palette
reads this to render draggable node options.

parameters.description: Filter to one of the 6 categories. Omit to get all 45 rows.
parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter to one of the 6 categories. Omit to get all 45 rows.
schema.title: Category
Response List Node Types Workflow Node Types Get

get.description: Poll run status. RLS scopes via enterprise_id GUC.
List approval gates for a run (pending + historical).

schema.title: Response List Workflow Approvals Workflow Runs  Run Id  Approvals Get

post.description:
Approve or reject the pending approval gate of a run.

Body: {decision: 'approve'|'reject', decision_note: str?}

Authorization: caller's role (X-User-Role from gateway-injected JWT)
must be in workflow_approvals.approver_roles for the pending gate.
If approver_user_id is set, X-User-ID must match exactly.

K-13 anti-IDOR: server cross-checks role + user_id against the
pending row — cannot approve gates outside your remit.

On 'approve': run resumes in background via resume_after_approval.
On 'reject':  run fails terminally; no resume.

get.description: Per-node execution detail. Ordered by sequence_order then started_at.

schema.title: Response List Workflow Run Nodes Workflow Runs  Run Id  Nodes Get

post.description:
Stop a run under human oversight (EU AI Act Art 14 / K-23): cancel the
run + fire saga compensation for already-executed impactful nodes.
Idempotent (K-13): stopping an already-cancelled run returns its state.

Create a folder under a workflow card. Optional parent_folder_id
for nested sub-folders Windows-style. Per anh's directive 'sắp xếp
ngăn nắp giống folder Win' 2026-05-15.

delete.description:
Soft-delete (sets status='archived'). Attached files stay; their
folder_id remains pointing here but FE filters out archived.

get.description:
List global workflow templates. Filter by dept_type and/or
industry for the 'New Workflow' picker UI.

parameters.description: P2-S15: filter by industry_vertical for cohort-style template discovery (AI-HSC-016).
parameters.name: industry

schema.description: P2-S15: filter by industry_vertical for cohort-style template discovery (AI-HSC-016).
schema.title: Industry
Response List Workflow Templates Workflow Templates Get

post.description: Emit a valid Temporal workflow YAML from a CDFL plan response.

Create workflow. department_id can be ANY dept in the caller's
workspace (Vingroup HQ user creating a workflow under a VinMart dept
is valid). enterprise_id is derived from the dept, not from caller.

Clone a global template → real workflow + nodes + edges for this enterprise.

The template's node `client_id`s ("n1", "n2"...) are replaced with
real UUIDs before insert, and the edges' `source_client_id` /
`target_client_id` are rewritten to point at those UUIDs.

Parse YAML → validate against mig 068 catalog → create workflow.

Failure modes (RFC 7807 400):
  - YAML parse error
  - Missing required top-level keys
  - Unknown node_type (not in mig 068 catalog)
  - Edge references undefined node id
  - Duplicate node id

get.description: Latest advisor review for a workflow, or {status:'never_run'}.

post.description: Kick a workflow self-evaluation (async). Poll GET …/advisor for the result.

get.description:
Return the stored BPMN 2.0 XML for a workflow (NULL = not authored;
FE opens an empty canvas). Workspace-scoped RLS via acquire_for_tenant.

put.description:
Store the BPMN XML authored in the builder. Validates it parses as
BPMN 2.0 first (400 on malformed) and returns the design summary so the
FE can show executable-coverage + ⚙ design-only badges immediately.

post.description:
Reverse of /bpmn/sync — project the Builder's workflow_nodes + edges INTO a
BPMN diagram so the BPMN tab renders the linear-Builder steps.

READ-ONLY on the steps (writes only workflows.bpmn_xml), so unlike sync
(BPMN→nodes *replace*) it never destroys builder work. Synthesises a start +
end event around the step graph so bpmn-js imports a valid single-pool
process. Idempotent — same steps → same diagram.

Project the stored BPMN diagram → workflow_nodes + workflow_edges.

Model A (anh chốt 2026-05-29): BPMN XML is the single source of truth, so
this is a full **replace** of the workflow's nodes/edges from the diagram.
The tree view + builder then render the BPMN-authored steps (with pool /
lane / event-definition metadata, mig 116). Idempotent — re-running with the
same XML yields the same graph.

Returns counts + the design summary (design-only elements get the
⚙ "chưa thực thi" badge; running them on the runner is a later step that
needs node_type_catalog_key reconcile — see WORKFLOW_BUILDER_REDESIGN.md).

schema.title: Response List Comments Workflows  Workflow Id  Comments Get

post.description:
Record a single customization event. Caller is responsible for
snapshotting (via separate /versions POST). v0 only logs the diff.

schema.title: Response Record Customization Workflows  Workflow Id  Customize Post

get.description:
3-tier document tree: workflow → step → {input[], output[], reference[]},
each requirement carrying its current document's status + version count.

post.description:
Simulate a sample record flowing through the workflow. Returns the nodes
visited + edges taken so the builder can highlight the path — decision nodes
are evaluated exactly as the runtime would, but nothing is executed.

schema.title: Response List Editors Workflows  Workflow Id  Editors Get

get.description:
Render the workflow + its nodes + edges as YAML for round-trip
portability. Joins workflow_nodes with node_type_catalog so the
exported `node_type` is the catalog key (not the row id).

delete.description: Release lock. Caller must echo the lock_token issued on acquire.

get.description: Return current lock holder or null if free. Auto-prunes expired.

schema.title: Response Check Lock Workflows  Workflow Id  Lock Get

post.description:
Acquire an edit lock. Conflict (409) if another user holds a
non-expired lock; same-user re-acquire refreshes acquired_at.

Start a new workflow run.

Validates every node in the workflow has a registered executor
before persisting the run row — fail-fast prevents starting runs
that will stall halfway. Returns 202 with the run_id; the caller
polls GET /workflow-runs/{id} for status.

K-13 idempotency: pass Idempotency-Key header; duplicate POSTs
return the existing run_id.

get.description: List recent runs of a workflow.

schema.title: Response List Workflow Runs Workflows  Workflow Id  Runs Get

get.description:
Aggregate báo cáo + thống kê for a workflow.

Returns:
  - file counts (total + per-step + per-document_kind)
  - cross-link counts (incoming + outgoing)
  - folder count
  - per-dept KPI snapshots (joins kpi_measurements via the workflow's
    department_id — caller's responsibility to ensure the dept has
    recent KPI computations).

Read-only aggregation; no Temporal needed (Phase 1 static digital twin).

Hierarchical tree: workflow → nodes (each with attached_documents[]) → edges.

FE renders this as the file-organizer view — user sees their workflow
as a tree of cards, each card showing the documents uploaded into it.

schema.title: Response List Workflow Versions Workflows  Workflow Id  Versions Get
